title: Debounced async search with stale-response cancellation
instruction: Implement the DebouncedSearch<T> controller in src/debouncedSearch.ts (a stub throws today). It debounces setQuery(query) by debounceMs before calling the injected fetcher, coalescing rapid calls into a single fetch of the latest query. It must be race-safe: if a newer query supersedes an in-flight request, the stale response is discarded (never applied to state). It exposes loading/error/results via the onChange callback, clears immediately without fetching on an empty query, surfaces fetch errors as error state (message), and dispose() cancels any pending timer and in-flight request. Do not change the public API or weaken the tests. A comprehensive failing test suite (fake timers + controllable promises) specifies the exact behavior.

criteria.guidance: Does it debounce correctly, discard stale responses under a newer query, and manage loading/error/empty states as specified?
Is the state machine clean and leak-free (timers cleared, requests aborted on dispose), with no race-prone shortcuts?
Is the control flow readable and are the request-generation / cancellation semantics obvious?
